reasoning: The resolution criteria require at least one person directly involved in the physical robbery to be apprehended by December 25, 2025, and not subsequently exonerated within a month. As of early November 2025 (well before the deadline), French police have already arrested and charged three individuals suspected of being direct participants in the physical robbery. The evidence against them is overwhelming, including DNA matching traces found on the getaway scooter, a museum window, and partial admissions of involvement from two of the suspects. The lead prosecutor has publicly stated that these individuals were part of the core team (the drivers and entry personnel). Given physical custody, strong forensic links tying them directly to the scene of the actual robbery, and partial confessions, the odds of all three being exonerated or proving to be merely accomplices (not direct physical perpetrators) are extremely remote. Therefore, this essentially resolves YES.
